title: SQL fundamentals for analysts
content: Learn SELECT, JOINs, GROUP BY, window functions. Practice on public datasets. Use official docs for your database (PostgreSQL/MySQL) and free exercises on sites that offer interactive SQL.

title: Python for data and automation
content: Core Python syntax, virtual environments, then pandas for tabular data: read_csv, groupby, merges. Build small scripts that automate a repetitive task. Read library docs and follow one structured tutorial series.

title: Intro to applied machine learning
content: Start with supervised learning workflow: train/validation split, metrics, cross-validation. Use scikit-learn for baselines before deep learning. Implement one end-to-end notebook from raw data to evaluation.

title: Frontend web development basics
content: HTML structure and accessibility, CSS layout (flex/grid), modern JavaScript, then component thinking with React. Build a small SPA that consumes a public API. Prefer official framework docs and MDN.

title: Backend APIs with Python
content: Design REST endpoints, request validation, persistence layer, and automated tests. Container basics help deployment stories. Document APIs with OpenAPI. Practice with a toy service and SQLite first.

title: DevOps fundamentals
content: Shell proficiency, git workflows, Docker images and compose, then a simple CI pipeline that runs tests. Monitor logs and health checks. Automate repetitive deploy steps with scripts.

title: Data visualization and storytelling
content: Choose chart types for the message, avoid chart junk, and practice executive summaries. Rebuild a dashboard from a sample dataset. Learn one tool deeply (e.g. spreadsheet + one BI tool).

title: Cloud fundamentals
content: Core services: compute, object storage, IAM, networking basics. Follow a well-architected overview. Use free tier sandboxes and destroy resources when done to avoid cost.

title: Git and collaboration
content: Branching, pull requests, resolving conflicts, meaningful commits. Contribute to a small open-source README fix or internal doc to practice the workflow.

title: Technical interview preparation
content: Practice explaining trade-offs, complexity, and testing strategy. Use STAR for behavioral stories. Do timed practice on whiteboard or doc, then compare with exemplar solutions.

title: Data engineering and ETL basics
content: Understand batch vs streaming, idempotent jobs, and schema evolution. Practice with a small pipeline: extract from CSV/API, transform in Python or SQL, load into a local warehouse. Learn dimensional modeling basics.

title: Application and cloud security fundamentals
content: Study OWASP Top 10, secure SDLC, secrets management, and least privilege. Practice threat modeling on a simple web app. Review cloud IAM and logging basics.

title: Product management essentials
content: Write crisp problem statements, define success metrics, and run lightweight discovery interviews. Learn prioritization frameworks and how to write user stories with acceptance criteria.

title: UX research and prototyping
content: Plan usability tests, create low-fi wireframes, iterate with feedback. Learn WCAG basics and keyboard navigation. Build a small design system for one project.

title: Mobile development patterns
content: Handle navigation, state, and platform differences. Profile startup time and memory. Practice offline-first patterns and secure storage of tokens.

title: Test automation strategy
content: Separate unit, integration, and E2E tests. Stabilize flaky tests with retries and better selectors. Mock external services in API tests. Integrate tests into CI with fast feedback.

title: Observability and reliability
content: Define SLIs and error budgets. Use structured logging, metrics, and traces. Write runbooks and practice blameless postmortems.

title: LLMs, prompting, and RAG
content: Learn prompt patterns, evaluation sets, and guardrails. Build a small RAG pipeline: chunk documents, embed, retrieve, and cite sources in answers. Measure hallucination rate on fixed questions.

title: Kubernetes essentials for developers
content: Pods, services, deployments, config maps, and secrets. Debug with kubectl logs and describe. Practice local clusters (minikube/kind) and health probes.

title: Smart contract safety and testing
content: Use established patterns, avoid reentrancy and overflow issues, and write unit tests with local chains. Read audit summaries and practice on testnets only.

title: Embedded C and debugging
content: Master pointers, interrupts, and memory-mapped I/O. Use a debugger and logic analyzer for timing issues. Follow MISRA-style discipline for safety-critical habits.

title: Financial analysis for analysts
content: Build three-statement links, sensitivity tables, and variance bridges. Reconcile numbers to source systems and document assumptions clearly.

title: Developer documentation craft
content: Structure docs with task-based topics, include runnable examples, and version docs with code. Use diagrams sparingly and test every procedure.

title: Game programming patterns
content: Separate update vs render loops, manage object pools, and profile frame time. Learn basic collision layers and animation state machines.

title: Customer onboarding and integrations
content: Reproduce customer issues with minimal examples, read API error codes systematically, and document workarounds. Balance speed with sustainable support processes.
